title: clinopodium menthifolium subsp. menthifolium
intro: Clinopodium menthifolium subsp. menthifolium, also known as Satureja montana subsp. montana, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, known for its small white or pink flowers and its culinary and medicinal uses. Clinopodium menthifolium subsp. menthifolium has been traditionally used for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections. The herbal preparations of this plant include tea, essential oil, and tincture, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other health conditions, as well as to promote relaxation and improve mental clarity.
